personal.name: Yusuf Ahmad
personal.headline: Computer Architecture, RTL & Hardware Security
personal.description: Interested in processor design, memory systems, AI accelerators, and cryptographic hardware for efficient next-generation computing.

overview.name: Computer Architecture & Memory Systems
overview.description: Exploring the design and organization of processors, memory hierarchies, and system-level architectures that power modern computing platforms.

overview.name: AI and Cryptographic Hardware Acceleration
overview.description: Investigating specialized hardware architectures for accelerating artificial intelligence workloads and cryptographic operations through efficient domain-specific computing.

overview.name: High-Performance & Energy-Efficient Computing
overview.description: Studying scalable computing systems that maximize performance while minimizing energy consumption through parallelism, optimization, and secure design.

research.title: RISC-V SoC with Hardware Accelerators
research.description: Design a complete RISC-V based System-on-Chip integrating custom RTL IPs, memory-mapped peripherals, and hardware accelerators through standardized interconnects.

research.title: Neural Network Hardware Accelerator
research.description: Develop a configurable hardware accelerator for machine learning inference, focusing on performance, scalability, and energy-efficient execution.

research.title: Out-of-Order RISC-V Processor
research.description: Implement an out-of-order RISC-V processor featuring dynamic scheduling, speculative execution, branch prediction, and advanced memory subsystems.

internship.title: VLSI Based IC Design
description: Completed an industrial internship focused on VLSI-based IC design methodologies, RTL development, FPGA implementation, and digital hardware design workflows. Implemented a low-power encryption system using FPGA and microcontroller co-design as the primary internship project.

Independently led the end-to-end design and verification of a lightweight FPGA-based encryption module (Low-Power LFSR XOR Encryption Core), implementing an SPI-driven XOR cipher with dynamically evolving LFSR key scheduling for secure sensor data transmission.

Developed synthesizable VHDL RTL for serial-to-parallel SPI data capture, dual-flip-flop clock synchronization for metastability mitigation, and cycle-accurate byte-level encryption; verified functionality using custom self-checking testbench in Vivado simulation environment.

Achieved post-implementation power estimates of ~26–35 mW with <1% LUT utilization (75 LUTs, 48 FFs) under Spartan-7 constraints, enabling single-cycle encryption latency per 8-bit payload and SPI throughput up to 10 MHz for real-time embedded communication workloads.

theses.title: Evaluation of RISC-V System-on-Chip (SoC) Interface Architecture
theses.description: Designed and implemented a RISC-V System-on-Chip (SoC) integrating a single-cycle processor with a ChaCha20 cryptographic accelerator through a memory-mapped interface. The project explored how processor instructions can control and coordinate dedicated hardware accelerators within a unified architecture. Developed in SystemVerilog and deployed on a Xilinx Artix-7 FPGA, the system successfully demonstrated processor-to-accelerator communication, memory transactions, and hardware-assisted encryption.

theses.title: Internet of Everything: A Study of Next-Generation Connected Systems
theses.description: Conducted an academic study exploring the Internet of Everything (IoE) paradigm, examining the integration of people, devices, data, and processes within large-scale connected environments. Investigated decentralized data processing, intelligent decision-making, cloud and edge computing integration, and the relationship between IoE and emerging technologies such as AI, IoT, and machine learning.

publications.title: Spartan-7 FPGA Based Implementation of ChaCha20 Accelerator for RISC-V Systems
publications.description: Implemented and evaluated a low-power ChaCha20 hardware accelerator at the RTL level. The design uses a serialized 32-bit datapath and memory-mapped interface to support efficient cryptographic acceleration in embedded SoC environments while minimizing dynamic power consumption.
publications.citation: Y. Ahmad and K. K. Singh, 'Spartan-7 FPGA Based Implementation of ChaCha20 Accelerator for RISC-V Systems', 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI), Greater Noida, (Delhi NCR), India, 2026, pp. 490-495, doi: 10.1109/IC3ECSBHI67834.2026.11469110. keywords: {Radio broadcasting;Frequency modulation;Field programmable gate arrays;Register transfer level;System-on-chip;Flip-flops;Central Processing Unit;Circuits and systems;Integrated circuits;Microprocessors;ChaCha20;hardware accelerator;low power design;RTL implementation;memory mapped interface},
publications.abstract: ChaCha20 is a stream cipher widely adopted in modern communication systems due to its simplicity and strong cryptographic properties. While software implementations are common, hardware acceleration is often required in embedded and resource constrained systems to meet performance and power constraints. This work presents a register transfer level implementation of a ChaCha20 encryption core designed with an emphasis on low dynamic power consumption. The proposed design employs a serialized 32 bit data path, reducing switching activity at the cost of increased latency, which is acceptable for low throughput and control oriented applications. The architecture is organized around a memory mapped interface to support integration as a peripheral accelerator in processor based systems. The design is described entirely at the RTL level and evaluated through synthesis and power analysis. Implementation results are used to examine the impact of architectural trade offs on power, timing, and resource utilization without modifying the underlying cryptographic algorithm.
publications.venue: 2026 2nd International Conference on Cognitive Computing in Engineering, Communications, Sciences and Biomedical Health Informatics (IC3ECSBHI)

publications.title: Low Power Encryption for IoT Devices using FPGA and Microcontroller
publications.description: Designed and evaluated a lightweight hardware encryption architecture for resource-constrained embedded systems. The VHDL-based design combines dynamic LFSR key evolution, SPI interfacing, and FPGA implementation to provide secure communication with low power and hardware overhead.
publications.citation: Y. Ahmad and K. K. Singh, 'Low Power Encryption for IoT Devices using FPGA and Microcontroller,' 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC), Sonbhadra, India, 2025, pp. 1009-1013, doi: 10.1109/CNC68716.2025.11484880. keywords: {Payloads;Field programmable gate arrays;Microcontrollers;System-on-chip;Flip-flops;Very large scale integration;Integrated circuits;Circuits;Microprocessors;Power dissipation;Encryption;FPGA;Spartan-7;VHDL;LFSR;XOR;SPI;Low-Power Design;Embedded Security},
publications.abstract: This paper presents the design and simulation of a low-power encryption module for secure data transmission in sensor-driven systems. The proposed lightweight encryption engine is described in VHDL, interfaced with a microcontroller through the SPI protocol, and synthesized using Xilinx Vivado with constraints targeting a Spartan-7 FPGA. The encryption scheme employs XOR-based logic with LFSR-driven key evolution and optional bit-position scrambling. Security, performance, and estimated power metrics, derived from Vivado synthesis and implementation reports, are discussed in relation to AES, DES, and RSA. Results indicate that the proposed design is suitable for energy-constrained embedded applications.
publications.venue: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Communication Networks and Computing (CNC)

vlsi.title: Memory-Mapped RISC-V SoC with Integrated ChaCha20 Hardware Accelerator
vlsi.overview: Single-cycle RISC-V SoC integrating a memory-mapped ChaCha20 hardware accelerator through processor-controlled communication.
vlsi.description: Designed and implemented a RISC-V System-on-Chip integrating a single-cycle processor with a ChaCha20 cryptographic accelerator through a memory-mapped interface. The architecture demonstrates processor-to-accelerator communication, memory transactions, execution control, and hardware-assisted encryption.

vlsi.title: Pipelined ChaCha20 Cryptographic Accelerator Core
vlsi.overview: Accelerator-oriented ChaCha20 architecture emphasizing modularity, scalability, and integration-ready subsystem organization.
vlsi.description: Developed an enhanced ChaCha20 accelerator architecture focused on subsystem decomposition, accelerator-oriented datapath organization, and future SoC integration. The design explored architectural tradeoffs involving throughput, latency, modularity, and implementation scalability.

vlsi.title: FPGA-Based ChaCha20 Encryption Core using SystemVerilog
vlsi.overview: RTL implementation of the ChaCha20 stream cipher using iterative quarter-round transformations and FSM-controlled execution.
vlsi.description: Implemented a synthesizable ChaCha20 encryption core featuring iterative quarter-round computation, FSM-controlled execution, and structured datapath organization. The project focused on hardware realization of modern cryptographic algorithms and accelerator-oriented RTL design.

vlsi.title: Modular AXI4-Lite Slave Peripheral with Transaction-Level Verification Infrastructure
vlsi.overview: AXI4-Lite slave architecture featuring distributed FSMs, protocol-compliant transactions, and transaction-oriented verification.
vlsi.description: Designed and verified a modular AXI4-Lite slave peripheral supporting memory-mapped communication, independent read/write channels, response-aware transaction handling, and byte-selective writes. Verification focused on randomized transaction execution and protocol compliance.

technical_specs.channel structure: Decomposed Independent Channels (AW, W, B, AR, R)

vlsi.title: CDC-Safe Asynchronous FIFO with Structured UVM-Based Verification Environment
vlsi.overview: Dual-clock FIFO architecture featuring Gray-code synchronization, metastability mitigation, and reusable UVM verification infrastructure.
vlsi.description: Designed a robust asynchronous FIFO for reliable data transfer across independent clock domains. The design employs Gray-code pointer synchronization and multi-stage synchronizers to mitigate metastability, complemented by a complete UVM-based verification environment for constrained-random testing and functional validation.

technical_specs.pointer scheduling: Binary Local Addressing / Gray-Coded Cross-Domain Transfer

vlsi.title: Low Power Encryption for IoT Devices using FPGA and Microcontroller
vlsi.overview: FPGA-based lightweight encryption engine featuring SPI deserialization, dynamic LFSR key evolution, and synchronization-aware data acquisition.
vlsi.description: Designed and implemented a lightweight encryption architecture for resource-constrained embedded systems. The design combines SPI communication, dynamic LFSR key evolution, XOR-based encryption, and FPGA deployment to achieve secure communication with low hardware overhead and reduced power consumption.

technical_specs.power profile: 0.026 W Total On-Chip Power (0.006 W Dynamic)

iot.title: Fleet Management using Smart Technology
iot.overview: Arduino-based smart traffic management prototype featuring dynamic signal control, traffic density monitoring, and adaptive energy-aware operation.
iot.description: Designed and developed a prototype smart traffic management system focused on real-time traffic monitoring and adaptive signal control. The project explored traffic density estimation using digital counters, Arduino-based signal control, dynamic timing adjustments, emergency response prioritization concepts, and adaptive energy management strategies for intelligent transportation systems.

technical_specs.algorithm support: 3-State Dynamic Gating (Energy Saver / One Side Green / Ratio Control)
technical_specs.detection interface: IR Traffic-Density Subsystem / GPS Telemetry Hooks
technical_specs.electrical profile: Parallel RGB Array Sync / Series 220-ohm Balancing
technical_specs.development suite: Arduino IDE / Proteus VSM Emulation Engine

communityOutreach.title: Children Rights: Empowering Slum Communities
description: Led a community outreach initiative focused on educating underprivileged children about fundamental rights, equality, education, and social responsibility. Organized awareness activities, educational sessions, and engagement programs aimed at empowering children from underserved communities through knowledge and community participation.

Formulated and executed a structured 8-week field action plan, designing creative educational modules and interactive camps to simplify complex concepts like constitutional equality, mutual respect, and fundamental child rights for marginalized youth.

Conducted grassroots field observations and stakeholder interviews to capture core socioeconomic variables regarding child labor and malnutrition analyzed community awareness gaps to synthesize actionable recommendations for localized welfare infrastructure under academic guidance.
